summary: A decision-ready plan for launching a credible, repeatable local chapter.

blocks.heading: Build the local front door to applied AI
blocks.body: A chapter lead becomes a visible facilitator and go-to resource for the local AI community.
citations: Local PDF · GenAI Collective Chapter Startup FAQ.pdf · chunk 05

blocks.heading: 180+ chapters, 40+ countries, 400+ organizers
blocks.body: A global community that includes everyone from solo founders to teams at the biggest AI labs in the world.

blocks.heading: Start with one repeatable event format
blocks.body: The network already runs Demo Nights, hackathons, private dinners, and workshops. Choose one format that matches the local audience and can repeat.
citations: https://newsletter.aicollective.com/ · Frequently Asked Questions · What’s The AI Collective itself?

blocks.heading: A four-week launch sequence
blocks.body: WorkshopLM recommendation derived from the chapter brief; confirm timing and required approvals with AI Collective HQ.
items: Confirm the lead team and chapter purpose
Secure the first format, venue, and partner
Open registration and activate local outreach
Host the first event and review the evidence
citations: Derived from approved Workshop brief · validate with AI Collective HQ

blocks.heading: Design for financial sustainability before launch
blocks.body: Treat a repeatable venue and partner model as a launch condition—not cleanup after the first event.
citations: Local PDF · GenAI Collective Chapter Startup FAQ.pdf · chunk 30

blocks.heading: Trust begins at registration
blocks.body: The Code of Conduct covers attendees, speakers, sponsors, and volunteers. The current privacy policy governs event registration data and media use.
blocks.citationLabel: AI Collective · Conduct and Privacy policies

blocks.heading: Make three decisions before announcing
blocks.body: The launch is credible when ownership, the first experience, and the operating partner are explicit.
items: Who owns the chapter and each launch workstream?
What is the first event format and target date?
Which venue or partner makes the cadence sustainable?
citations: Derived from approved Workshop brief · validate with AI Collective HQ
blocks.citationLabel: Derived decision frame · HQ validation required

blocks.heading: Approve a 30-day chapter launch sprint
blocks.body: Use week one to confirm owners and HQ requirements. Do not announce until the first format, date, venue, and trust obligations have named owners.
citations: Local PDF · GenAI Collective Chapter Startup FAQ.pdf
blocks.citationLabel: WorkshopLM recommendation · grounded in chapter brief
